article_meta.title: Supreme Court ruling against Trump’s tariffs is unlikely to mean an end to trade policy chaos 1 of 3 | Containers are st

reasoning: The summary avoids the article’s more loaded framing (e.g., “stunning rebuke,” “chaotic,” “mess”) and uses measured, neutral language. It attributes actions and plans without adopting emotive or charged descriptors. Perspectives are represented proportionally, and claims are supported by the article.

custom_reasoning.spin: Uses neutral verbs (ruled, responded, exploring) and avoids loaded terms used in the article.
custom_reasoning.unsubstantiated_claims: All assertions mirror the article’s reported facts and attributions.
custom_reasoning.opinion_as_fact: Evaluative statements are either avoided or framed as expectations/warnings from analysts.
custom_reasoning.sensationalism: The tone is measured; replaces “stunning rebuke” and “chaotic/mess” with neutral phrasing.
custom_reasoning.mudslinging: No personal or ad hominem remarks.
custom_reasoning.mind_reading: No claims about intentions or mental states.
custom_reasoning.slant: Summarizes multiple angles (legal tools, EU reaction, refunds, analysts) without favoring one side.
custom_reasoning.bias_by_omission: Includes major perspectives present in the article, including administration, analysts, and EU concerns.
custom_reasoning.subjective_adjectives: Minimal and neutral adjectives (e.g., “significant,” “lengthy”) used descriptively.
custom_reasoning.word_choice: Avoids politically charged labels; uses standard policy terminology.
custom_reasoning.negativity_bias: Notes uncertainty consistent with the article without amplifying it.
custom_reasoning.elite_populist_bias: Treats official and expert sources similarly; no undue deference.
custom_reasoning.framing_neutrality: More neutral than the source; strips emotive framing and metaphors.
custom_reasoning.perspective_completeness: Reflects administration actions, legal context, international response, and business/analyst concerns proportionally.
